channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders have spurred more than 130 lawsuits, but one related to TikTok barely generates a peep.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini And Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GOP senators push ahead on Trump's tax cuts package, punting big decisions for later
item.description: Senate Republicans insist they are pushing ahead on President Donald Trump’s big bill of tax breaks and spending cuts this week.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump is set to announce 'reciprocal' tariffs in a risky move that could reshape the economy
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to launch a barrage of so-called reciprocal tariffs on friend and foe alike.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration halts dozens of research grants at Princeton University
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has halted dozens of research grants at Princeton University, the latest Ivy League school to see its federal money threatened in a pressure campaign targeting the nation’s top universities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal, medical cannabis fix-it legislation in the works at Minnesota Legislature
item.description: Sen. Scott Dibble, DFL-Minneapolis, is sponsoring several of those cleanup bills for both recreational cannabis and changes to medical marijuana rules. He joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer ahead of committee hearings Thursday to talk about what the proposed alterations entail.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: April Fools' Day in politics: On April 1, lawmakers trade punchlines instead of policy
item.description: Politicians used April Fools' Day to take jabs at their political enemies, including Democratic Colorado Gov. Jared Polis, who took shots at President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey Sen. Booker presses his marathon speech against Trump's agenda past 20 hours and counting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker held the Senate floor with a marathon speech that lasted all night and into Tuesday afternoon in a feat of endurance to show Democrats’ objections to President Donald Trump’s sweeping actions.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans torpedo Johnson effort to block proxy voting for new parents
item.description: The House GOP battle over proxy voting for new parents escalated to a full-blown procedural war Tuesday that will have ramifications for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) ability to control his razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's pick for Joint Chiefs chairman vows to be apolitical and addresses Signal chat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s nominee to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine, told senators Tuesday that he understands he is an unknown and unconventional nominee — but that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems ready to let religious groups opt out of unemployment compensation laws
item.description: The case was brought by a chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin, which says that it should be able to opt out of the mandatory state unemployment compensation system.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin voters to choose top education official and consider voter ID amendment
item.description: Wisconsin is electing its top education official, who will guide policies affecting K-12 schools during President Donald Trump’s second term. Tuesday’s vote pits the teachers union-backed incumbent Jill Underly against Republican-supported critic Brittany Kinser.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Inside a Coast Guard border crackdown
item.description: Chief Peter Nelson, officer in charge for Coast Guard Station San Diego, described escalating confrontations with smugglers.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about your rights when crossing the border
item.description: U.S. citizens may face questions if they're traveling with visa holders or living with individuals who are in the U.S. illegally, Nasseri says.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: Just 38 percent of respondents said they approve of Trump's approach to trade negotiations with other countries.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Alien Enemies Act controversy: A timeline
item.description: Congress originally passed the Alien Enemies Act of 1798 as the U.S. was poised to go to war with France.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
